aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom
description_wiki: London is the capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom, with a population of 9.1 million people in 2024.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: private school in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: Georgetown Day School (GDS) is an independent coeducational PK-12 school located in Washington, D.C. The school educates 1,075 elementary, middle, and high school students in northwestern Washington, D.C. Tori Jueds is the incoming Head of School.

description: U.S. Navy federal service academy in the Annapolis, Maryland area
description_wiki: The United States Naval Academy (USNA, Navy, or Annapolis) is a federal service academy adjacent to Annapolis, Maryland.

description: private research university in Medford/Somerville, Massachusetts
description_wiki: Tufts University is a private research university in Medford and Somerville, Massachusetts, United States, with additional facilities in Boston and Grafton, as well as Talloires, France.

entity: Fletcher School of Law and Diplomacy
aliases: Fletcher School of Law and Diplomacy
description: Tufts University school of international relations and law
description_wiki: The Fletcher School of Law and Diplomacy is the graduate school for international relations at Tufts University in Medford, Massachusetts, United States.

description: private research university in Boston, Massachusetts, United States
description_wiki: Boston University (BU) is a private research university in Boston, Massachusetts, United States.

description: United States Army post outside Columbus, Georgia, previously known as Fort Moore
description_wiki: Fort Benning (formerly Fort Moore from 2023–25) is a United States Army post in the Columbus, Georgia, area.

description: reconnaissance of the area bordering coastal and ocean areas
description_wiki: Amphibious reconnaissance consists of ground and naval reconnaissance in the littoral area bordering coastal or ocean areas.

description: private liberal arts college in Worcester, Massachusetts, U.S.
description_wiki: The College of the Holy Cross is a private Jesuit liberal arts college in Worcester, Massachusetts, United States.

aliases: Gulf War 2/battle of Ramadi/battle of Faluya
description: 2003–2011 war after an American-led invasion
description_wiki: The Iraq War (Arabic: حرب العراق, romanized: ḥarb al-ʿirāq), also referred to as the Second Gulf War, was a protracted armed conflict in Iraq from 2003 to 2011.

entity: United States Marine Corps Basic School
surface_forms: United States Marine Corps Basic School
description: US Marine Corps school for new officers and warrant officers
description_wiki: The Basic School (TBS) is where all newly commissioned and appointed (for warrant officers) United States Marine Corps officers are taught the basics of being an "Officer of Marines." The Basic School is located in Stafford County, Virginia to the south-west of the Marine Corps Base Quantico complex.

description: newspaper in Springfield, Virginia
description_wiki: Marine Corps Times (ISSN 1522-0869) is a newspaper serving active, reserve and retired United States Marine Corps personnel and their families, providing news, information and analysis as well as community and lifestyle features, educational supplements, and resource guides.

description: city in Al Anbar Governorate, Iraq
description_wiki: Ramadi (Arabic: ٱلرَّمَادِي Ar-Ramādī; IPA: [r.ra.maː.diː]; also formerly rendered as Rumadiyah or Rumadiya) is a city in central Iraq, about 110 kilometers (68 mi) west of Baghdad and 50 kilometers (31 mi) west of Fallujah.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: US Marine Corps base in Jacksonville, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune ( lə-ZHURN or  lə-ZHOON) is a 246-square-mile (640 km2) United States military training facility in Jacksonville, North Carolina.

description: battalion of the United States Marine Corps
description_wiki: 1st Battalion, 1st Marines (1/1) is an infantry battalion in the United States Marine Corps based out of Camp Pendleton, California, consisting of anywhere from 800 to 2,000 Marines and Sailors, but the number fluctuates depending on the battalion's mission.

description: military unit
description_wiki: 1st Battalion, 8th Marines (1/8) is an infantry marines battalion in the United States Marine Corps (USMC) based out of Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune, North Carolina.

description: US Marine Corps base near Oceanside, California, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton is the major West Coast base of the United States Marine Corps and is one of the largest Marine Corps bases in the United States.

description: active United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 82nd Airborne Division ("All American")  is a United States Army airborne infantry division that specializes in parachute assault, based in Fort Bragg, North Carolina.

description: American private military company
description_wiki: Constellis, formerly Blackwater, is an American private military contractor founded on December 26, 1997, by former Navy SEAL officer Erik Prince.

description: city in the province of Al Anbar, Iraq
description_wiki: Fallujah (Arabic: ٱلْفَلُّوجَة al-Fallūjah [el.fɐl.ˈluː.dʒɐ]) is a city in Al Anbar Governorate, Iraq.

description: daily broadsheet newspaper in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The Washington Post (locally known as The Post and, informally, WaPo or WP) is an American daily newspaper published in Washington, D.C. It is the most widely circulated newspaper in the Washington metropolitan area and is considered a newspaper of record in the United States.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: United States Army leadership training course
